exemplar.title: To what extent do the characteristics, diagnosis and treatment of ADHD vary in adolescent boys and girls?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the characteristics, diagnosis and treatment of ADHD vary in adolescent boys and girls?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused and maintained throughout, demonstrating strong topic communication and connection to discussion.
Sections are consistently signposted and aligned with the research question, ensuring an effective thread from introduction to conclusion.
weaknesses: There is no explicit methodology section or search strategy, resulting in limited transparency and justification for source selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the essay excels at framing a focused research question and sustaining a coherent narrative (two strands at the highest band), the absence of a transparent, systematic methodology (one strand at the lowest band) prevents it from achieving the top mark. An overall score of 4/6 accurately reflects strong focus and communication tempered by a weak methodology.

strengths: Accurate use of core ADHD terminology (e.g., âexternalising behavioursâ, âoperant conditioningâ) and coherent integration of physiological concepts such as dopamine pathways.
Solid grasp of key ADHD concepts (inattention, hyperactivity, masking, comorbidity) demonstrated through use of empirical studies.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on nonâscholarly sources (popular media, organisational webpages) weakens academic rigour and depth of application.
Occasional conceptual imprecision (e.g., unqualified causal claims about ADHD development) indicates inconsistent conceptual accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows generally accurate subject knowledge and mostly correct terminology (band 3â4 descriptors) but is undermined by reliance on secondary sources and occasional inaccuracies. A score of 2/6 reflects competent knowledge that falls short of the fully developed, critically applied standard.

strengths: Clear, coherent analysis comparing findings across studies and linking them back to diagnostic and treatment implications.
Reasoned argument structure with some critical evaluation of gender-skewed samples and treatment recommendations.
weaknesses: Overall research base is limited by the prevalence of nonâacademic web articles, restricting depth and relevance.
Analysis often remains descriptive, with limited probing of methodologies, effect sizes, or contradictory data, and evaluation stays at surface level.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit at the lower edge of the 4â6 band: research is partially relevant, analysis is clear but descriptive, and evaluation is reasoned but not deeply critical. A mark of 3/12 appropriately acknowledges this midâband performance while reflecting the need for more rigorous, in-depth critical thinking.

strengths: All required structural elements are present (title page, contents, section headings, references, word count), supporting reader navigation.
MLA referencing style is applied consistently and pagination is correctly used.
weaknesses: Minor formatting lapses (line-breaks in URLs, occasional in-text citation lapses) interrupt the visual consistency.
Justification for the choice of assessment tools and some heading hierarchies could be more explicitly explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is solid: it meets all structural conventions (band 3â4) but has small lapses that prevent a flawless execution. A score of 2/4 reflects competent structure and formatting with minor errors.

strandSelections.reasoning: Subject-specific terms such as âexternalising behaviours,â âMANCOVA,â and âoperant conditioningâ are used frequently and mostly correctly, showing familiarity with psychological vocabulary.  Nonetheless, occasional inaccuracies (e.g., suggesting ADHD may be 1developed due to psychological traumad without qualification) and inconsistent statistical detail indicate that conceptual precision is not fully consistent.  This mostly accurate but imperfect application matches the 3â4 band.

Pagination, heading hierarchy and general formatting are correctly applied, and MLA referencing is mostly consistent.  Minor issues (line-breaks in URLs, occasional in-text citation lapses) prevent a flawless presentation but do not impede readability.  These small lapses place the work solidly within the competent 3â4 band, meriting a 3.

The student demonstrates a solid grasp of core ADHD concepts (inattention, hyperactivity, masking, comorbidity) and integrates two recent empirical studies to illustrate gender contrasts.  However, sizeable reliance on secondary, non-scholarly sources (e.g., WebMD, Forbes, Medium, ChatGPT) dilutes academic rigour and limits depth of application.  Knowledge is generally accurate but not always synthesised critically, placing performance firmly in the middle band rather than the highest.

There is no explicit methodology section explaining how sources were located or why particular studies were chosen; the selection process therefore lacks transparency and justification.  The essay draws on a narrow range of peer-reviewed research (primarily two focal studies) supplemented by numerous popular-media or organisational webpages, revealing minimal evidence of informed, systematic source selection.  As a result, the methodology is limited in both range and rigour, aligning with the 1â2 band.

The title, abstract elements and opening paragraphs immediately establish ADHD, the gender lens and the adolescent focus, allowing the reader to understand both scope and purpose without ambiguity.  Throughout the essay the student repeatedly signposts how each section (symptom presentation, diagnosis, treatment) links back to gender differences, maintaining a coherent thread.  Occasional digressions into broad definitions (e.g., generic DSM-5 symptom lists) are brief and do not obscure the central purpose.  Overall, the topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with a clear and appropriate focus sustained from introduction to conclusion.

The essay incorporates several current studies and official statistics that are generally relevant to the research question, and the chosen qualitative and quantitative papers align with the gender focus.  However, the prevalence of non-academic web articles means the overall research base is adequate rather than extensive.  Consequently, the work meets the descriptor for 4adequate research partially relevantd at the lower end of the 4â6 band.

The research question â âTo what extent do the characteristics, diagnosis and treatment of ADHD vary in adolescent boys and girls?â â is specific, manageable and explicitly drives the organisation of the paper.  Each major heading mirrors an element of the question (manifestations, diagnostic issues, treatment), demonstrating a strong and consistent connection between the stated focus and subsequent discussion.  The wording avoids unnecessary breadth, targeting a single developmental period (adolescence) and three clearly delineated constructs, thereby meeting the descriptor for a well-focused and effectively connected research question.

All prescribed structural elements (title page, contents, section headings, references, word count) are present and logically organised.  The progression from introduction through literature review, study analysis, and conclusion follows EE conventions and supports reader comprehension, satisfying the 3â4d band for structure appropriateness.

The student advances a coherent discussion about diagnostic bias and presents some evaluation of study limitations (e.g., gender-skewed samples).  Nevertheless, evaluation often remains surface-level, and broader conclusions (e.g., need for gender-specific treatment) are asserted more than critically justified.  Argumentation is therefore reasoned but only partially critical, fitting the mid-band description.

Analytical sections compare findings across the two spotlighted studies and link them to practical diagnostic issues, demonstrating clear but limited analysis.  Many passages summarise literature without probing methodological strengths, effect sizes or contradictory data, so conclusions, while reasonable, are not comprehensively evidenced.  This represents clear but mainly descriptive analysis consistent with the lower edge of the 4â6 band.

annotations.comment: The list of assessment tools is comprehensive but lacks justification for their selection. Briefly explain why each measure is appropriate for this study.
The historical context regarding DSM-IV to DSM-5 evolution is well explained, demonstrating accurate use of terminology and conceptual understanding.
The subheading âWhy Girls are Underdiagnosedâ clearly aligns with a component of the research question, enhancing focus and readability.
The opening statistic of 129 million adolescents lacks contextualization of its sourceâs methods and date. Critically appraise and situate such claims to strengthen academic rigour.
The summary of DSM-5 criteria for inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity is accurate and demonstrates solid subject knowledge and terminology usage.
Citing ChatGPT as a source is inappropriate for academic research. Replace with peer-reviewed or primary sources to maintain scholarly standards.
The title is clearly stated at the start of the report, meeting formatting requirements and immediately orienting the reader to the investigationâs focus.
The research question is prominently presented and precisely mirrors the investigationâs scope, ensuring a well-focused and effectively connected discussion.
A word count is provided on the title page, fulfilling the formatting requirement and confirming compliance with the word limit.
There is no explicit methodology section detailing how sources were identified or selected. Include a clear description of the search strategy and selection criteria to enhance transparency.
The discussion of ADHDâs life impacts provides general overview but lacks direct critical engagement with the cited meta-analysis methodology and potential limitations.
The transition to gender variation is clearly signposted, maintaining a coherent thread that connects back to the research question.
The section heading effectively marks the shift to specific manifestations, reflecting a well-structured approach and adherence to extended essay conventions.
The table of contents is comprehensive and correctly formatted, facilitating navigation and demonstrating a clear structure.
The prevalence of comorbidities is noted but not critically evaluated. Discuss the sourceâs methodology and potential sampling biases to deepen analysis.
The claim about ADHD subtypes shifting to dimensional variables is asserted without citation of specific research; provide references and outline their methods.
Assertions about grade patterns are anecdotal and lack empirical support. Incorporate quantitative data or specific studies to substantiate these claims.
The link between dopamine pathways and behavioural outcomes demonstrates strong conceptual understanding and integrates physiological context effectively.
Discussion relies on generically cited sources without scholarly rigour. Replace or supplement with peer-reviewed studies to strengthen academic credibility.
The âAcademic Impactâ subheading is appropriately nested under its parent section, reflecting a clear and logical heading hierarchy.
The transition to âAnalyzing Studiesâ is clearly signposted, guiding the reader into the empirical analysis segment.
The description of Study 1 directly addresses clinical characteristics, effectively linking empirical evidence to the research question.
The limitation of uneven sample sizes is noted but not fully explored. Expand on how this imbalance affects validity and generalizability of the findings.
The qualitative themes are clearly articulated, demonstrating skillful synthesis and effective presentation of participantsâ experiences.
The integration of qualitative and quantitative findings would be strengthened by explicitly linking themes across both studies to highlight convergences and divergences.
The comparative discussion synthesizes insights from both studies effectively, reflecting higherâorder critical evaluation.
A raw hyperlink appears instead of a full citation entry. Convert to a complete bibliographic reference to uphold formatting standards.
Interpretation of MANOVA and tâtest results is well presented, demonstrating clear understanding of statistical methods and their relevance.
The argument for tailoring treatment by gender demonstrates applied understanding of both pharmacological and psychosocial interventions.
The bibliography begins on a new page with a clear 'Work Cited' heading, satisfying formatting conventions for reference sections.
Conclusions about IQ differences would benefit from reporting specific effect sizes and confidence intervals to support the interpretation.
The âTreatment Approachesâ heading marks a clear structural shift, maintaining organizational coherence through to the conclusion.
General treatment strategies are described but lack citation of clinical guidelines or outcome studies; reference authoritative sources to substantiate these recommendations.
The conclusion succinctly restates the research question and synthesizes key findings, providing a clear and focused closure to the investigation.
Future research recommendations are broad. Suggest specific study designs or variables to address identified gaps and strengthen practical relevance.
Reference entries exhibit inconsistencies in italics and URL formatting. Standardize according to MLA conventions to improve presentation.
The inclusion of diverse sources such as statistical overviews and peerâreviewed articles indicates an effort to triangulate data and support claims.
